title: Odes Of Anacreon - Ode LXXIII.
content:
Awhile I bloomed, a happy flower,
    Till love approached one fatal hour,
    And made my tender branches feel
    The wounds of his avenging steel.
    Then lost I fell, like some poor willow
    That falls across the wintry billow!
meaning:
"Odes Of Anacreon - Ode LXXIII." is a quintessential example of Thomas Moore's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
